- Gudde Mëtteg.
- Gudde Mëtteg, Herr Weber. Gëtt et dem Max do erëm besser?
- E gesäit gutt aus.
- Jo, mir ware virun zwee Deeg beim Deierendokter. E gudde Medikament an der Tränk gutt ze wicken.
- Da sinn ech awer frou.
- So Mathieu, fäerts du do?
- Ma de François huet mech gefrot, fir seng Breifboîte zweemol pro Woch ze maachen.
- Hien ass dräi Wochen an der Vakanz. Ech gi nach seng Planzen netzen.
- Ech hat dat lescht Joer vergiess. Ech hoffen, si sinn net futti.
- Dat hei ass wierklech eng flott Géigend. Et sinn ëmmer déi selwecht Leit, déi hei spadséieren, an d’Mathekanner kommen no op de Spillplatz.
- Et ass frësch a sécher hei. Du brauchs do wierklech keen Sürschen ze maachen.
- Hei ass nach ni eppes geschitt.
- Ech muss muer op Sandweiler an d’Kontroll. Mäi Brudder ass lescht Joer mat senger Mautor duerchgefall.
- Ech kucken léiwer eng Kéier, ob alles funktionéiert.
- Wéi wësst du, wéini déi lescht Kontroll war?
- Good afternoon.
- Good afternoon, Mr. Weber. Is Max feeling better again?
- He looks good.
- Yes, we were at the veterinarian two days ago. A good medicine in the drink helps well.
- I am pleased to hear that.
- So Mathieu, are you ready there?
- But François asked me to do his mailboxes twice a week.
- He is on vacation for three weeks. I will still water his plants.
- I forgot last year. I hope they are not spoiled.
- This place is really a nice area. It’s always the same people strolling here, and the math cats come to the playground.
- It is fresh and safe here. You really don’t need to do any gardening there.
- Nothing has ever happened here.
- I have to go to Sandweiler tomorrow for the inspection. My brother failed with his driving test last year.
- I’d rather check once to see if everything is working.
- How do you know when the last inspection was?

- Sou, a wou muss ech dann nun fueren?
- De Philipp huet geschriwwen an der Rüdeleglise, déi éischt Strooss lénks.
- Jo, mee kuck, dat ass eng Sackgass.
- Dofir muss ech riets.
- Ach, dat wierklech Rüdeleglise.
- Jo, mee hei déi nächst Strooss lénks, do si mam Këschtwee.
- Pass op, hei ass riets Véierfahrt.
- Ups! Mach loos, du däerfs dech just mat Risse schéieren.
- Ho, déi blöd Vitesslimiten, et feelt just nach Radar hei.
- Hei, kuck, ech mengen hei ass et, mee hei derfir net parken.
- Jo, de Philipp huet jo geschriwwen, e puer honnert Meter méi wäit kann ech parken.
- So, and where should I drive to now?
- Philipp wrote in the roundabout, the first street on the left.
- Yes, but look, that’s a dead end.
- Therefore I must turn right.
- Oh, that really is a roundabout.
- Yes, but over there the next street on the left, there they are with the box tree.
- Watch out, here is a right four-way intersection.
- Oops! Move along, you may only get by with a scrape.
- Oh, those stupid speed limits, there’s just no radar here.
- Look here, I think here it is, but for that you may not park here.
- Yes, Philipp wrote, a few hundred meters further I may park.

- Wéi heescht Dir?
- Mäi Numm ass Sophie.
- Vu wou kënnt Dir?
- Ech kommen aus Lëtzebuerg.
- Wéi al sidd Dir?
- Ech sinn 25 Joer al.
- Wou wunnt Dir?
- Ech wunnen zu Esch.
- Sidd Dir Schwäizer?
- Nee, ech sinn Lëtzebuergerin.
- An Dir?
- Ech sinn Däitsch.
- Wéi geet et Iech?
- Et geet mir gutt, merci.
- Wéi laang léiert Dir schonn Lëtzebuergesch?
- Ech léieren zënter engem Mount.
- Wat maacht Dir berufflech?
- Ech sinn Enseignantin.
- Hutt Dir Kanner?
- Jo, ech hunn zwou Kanner.
- Wéi heeschen Är Kanner?
- Si heeschen Tom a Léa.
- Wou schafft Dir?
- Ech schaffen an enger Schoul zu Lëtzebuerg-Stad.
- Wat ass Äre Lieblingshobby?
- Mäi Lieblingshobby ass Musek lauschteren.
- Hutt Dir eng Famill?
- Jo, ech sinn bestuet.
- Wéi laang sidd Dir schonn bestuet?
- Ech sinn zënter fënnef Joer bestuet.
- Wéi vill Sproochen schwätzt Dir?
- Ech schwätzen dräi Sproochen: Lëtzebuergesch, Däitsch a Franséisch.
- Wéi laang bleift Dir nach zu Lëtzebuerg?
- Ech bleiwe nach e Mount hei.
- Wéi ass d’Wieder haut?
- Et ass sonneg a waarm.
- Wat iesst Dir gär?
- Ech iessen gär Nuddelen.
- Hutt Dir eng Lieblingsplaz zu Lëtzebuerg?
- Jo, ech ginn gär an de Park.
- Wéi oft gitt Dir an de Park?
- Ech ginn all Dag an de Park.
- Wat maacht Dir am Weekend?
- Am Weekend besichen ech meng Frënn.
- Wéi heescht Äre beschte Frënd?
- Mäi beschte Frënd heescht Marc.
- Wéi laang kennt Dir hie scho?
- Ech kennen hie scho fënnef Joer.
- Wéi oft schwätzt Dir mat him?
- Mir schwätzen all Dag.
- Hutt Dir eng Hausdéier?
- Jo, ech hunn eng Kaz.
- Wéi heescht Är Kaz?
- Si heescht Mimi.
- Wat fir eng Faarf huet Är Kaz?
- Si ass schwaarz a wäiss.
- Wéi vill Leit sinn an Ärer Famill?
- Mir sinn fënnef Leit an der Famill.
- Wéi heescht Ären Elter?
- Mäi Papp heescht Jean a meng Mamm heescht Marie.
- Wéi laang léiert Dir schonn Lëtzebuergesch?
- Ech léieren zënter engem Mount.
- Wéi fënnt Dir d’Sprooch?
- Ech fannen se schéin a schwéier.
- Wéi oft übt Dir Lëtzebuergesch?
- Ech üben all Dag.
- Wéi laang dauert Äre Cours?
- Mäi Cours dauert zwou Stonnen.
- Wéi vill Leit sinn an Ärer Klass?
- Mir sinn zéng Leit an der Klass.
- Wéi heescht Äre Léierin?
- Mäi Léierin heescht Madame Müller.
- Wéi oft gitt Dir an d’Schoul?
- Ech ginn fënnef Mol d’Woch an d’Schoul.
- Wéi laang dauert Är Rees op d’Schoul?
- Meng Rees dauert 20 Minutten.
- Wéi gitt Dir op d’Schoul?
- Ech ginn mam Bus op d’Schoul.
- Wéi laang bleift Dir nach zu Lëtzebuerg?
- Ech bleiwe nach e Mount hei.
- Wéi fënnt Dir d’Stad?
- Ech fannen d’Stad flott a lieweg.
- Wat maacht Dir gär an der Fräizäit?
- Ech liesen gär Bicher.
- Hutt Dir eng Lieblingsbuch?
- Jo, mäi Lieblingsbuch ass „D’Rees“.
- Wéi heescht Äre Lieblingsfilm?
- Mäi Lieblingsfilm heescht „D’Liewen“.
- Wéi oft gitt Dir an de Kino?
- Ech ginn all Mount an de Kino.
- Wéi heescht Äre Lieblingssänger?
- Mäi Lieblingssänger heescht Tom.
- Hutt Dir eng Lieblingssport?
- Jo, ech spillen gär Tennis.
- Wéi oft spillt Dir Tennis?
- Ech spillen zwou Mol d’Woch Tennis.
- Wéi laang léiert Dir schonn Lëtzebuergesch?
- Ech léieren zënter engem Mount.
- Wéi fënnt Dir d’Sprooch?
- Ech fannen se schéin a schwéier.
- Wéi oft übt Dir Lëtzebuergesch?
- Ech üben all Dag.

- What is your name?
- My name is Sophie.
- And where are you from?
- I come from Luxembourg.
- How old are you?
- I am 25 years old.
- Where do you live?
- I live in Esch.
- Are you Swiss?
- No, I am Luxembourgish.
- And you?
- I am German.
- How are you?
- I am well, thank you.
- How long have you been learning Luxembourgish?
- I have been learning for a month.
- What do you do professionally?
- I am a teacher.
- Do you have children?
- Yes, I have two children.
- What are your children’s names?
- They are named Tom and Léa.
- Where do you work?
- I work in a school in Luxembourg City.
- What is your favorite hobby?
- My favorite hobby is listening to music.
- Do you have a family?
- Yes, I am married.
- How long have you been married?
- I have been married for five years.
- How many languages do you speak?
- I speak three languages: Luxembourgish, German, and French.
- How long will you stay in Luxembourg?
- I will stay here for another month.
- How is the weather today?
- It is sunny and warm.
- What do you like to eat?
- I like to eat noodles.
- Do you have a favorite place in Luxembourg?
- Yes, I like going to the park.
- How often do you go to the park?
- I go to the park every day.
- What do you do on the weekend?
- On the weekend I visit my friends.
- What is your best friend's name?
- My best friend's name is Marc.
- How long have you known him?
- I have known him for five years.
- How often do you talk to him?
- We talk every day.
- Do you have a pet?
- Yes, I have a cat.
- What is your cat’s name?
- Her name is Mimi.
- What color is your cat?
- She is black and white.
- How many people are in your family?
- There are five people in the family.
- What are your parents’ names?
- My father is named Jean and my mother is named Marie.
- How long have you been learning Luxembourgish?
- I have been learning for a month.
- How do you find the language?
- I find it beautiful and difficult.
- How often do you practice Luxembourgish?
- I practice every day.
- How long is your course?
- My course lasts two hours.
- How many people are in your class?
- There are ten people in the class.
- What is your teacher’s name?
- My teacher’s name is Madame Müller.
- How often do you go to school?
- I go to school five times a week.
- How long is your trip to school?
- My trip to school takes 20 minutes.
- How do you get to school?
- I take the bus to school.
- How long will you stay in Luxembourg?
- I will stay here for another month.
- How do you find the city?
- I find the city nice and lively.
- What do you like to do in your free time?
- I like to read books.
- Do you have a favorite book?
- Yes, my favorite book is “The Journey.”
- What is the name of your favorite movie?
- My favorite movie is “The Life.”
- How often do you go to the cinema?
- I go to the cinema every month.
- What is the name of your favorite singer?
- My favorite singer is Tom.
- Do you have a favorite sport?
- Yes, I enjoy playing tennis.
- How often do you play tennis?
- I play tennis twice a week.
- How long have you been learning Luxembourgish?
- I have been learning for a month.
- How do you find the language?
- I find it beautiful and difficult.
- How often do you practice Luxembourgish?
- I practice every day.
- Thank you very much!
- You’re welcome!

- Hey Philippe, super Party!
- Merci, Dani. Schéin, dech mol erëmzegesinn.
- Jo, de schoch, mir et färet einfach, dech eréinanzekommen.
- Also?
- Jo, ech hunn net opgepasst, a sinn an de Sandhänter diereragefuer.
- Eegentlech huet keen mech gesinn.
- Oh, mei, mee lobe as du jo hei.
- Jo, an ech muss soen, du hues wierklech e flott Appartement.
- Merci, ech si ganz frou hei.
- Hues de meng zwee Matbewunner scho kennegeleiert?
- Jo, zwee ganz sympatesch Kärelen.
- Sinn deng Noperen och esou fein?
- Jo, bis elo hat ech nach kee Problem.
- Dann hues du Chance.
- Mäin Noper mëscht mech gackeg.
- Hie kennt nëmmen zielen, an da mëscht hie vill Kameidi am Gang.
- Hues du dann net geklaméiers?
- Dach, et ass dann nëmme, déi schrouesch, an de Nu passt hien erëm net méi op.
- Mee en enge Mound plënnert hien.
- Dann hunn ech endlech meng Rou.
- Wievees, vleicht ass den nächste Noper nach méi schlëm.
- Villmols merci, fir däin Optimismus.
- Salut dir zwee!
- Hey Jennifer, an?
- Hues de dem Philippe sein Zëmmer scho gesinn?
- Nee, nach net.
- Kann ech denker jo kucke goen?
- Jo, sécher. Komm mat.
- Elo hat de Speil vergiess.
- Hei as däi Kado.
- Eischtrop hannescht leikan.
- Flott, merci.
- Ech stellen se am beschten hei op den Dësch.
- Nee, stellt se op't Regal. Do gesäit ee se besser.
- A rekel den Dësch e bësse no lénks. Dann hues de méi Plaz.
- Okay, Chefin.
- Wat kann ech soen? Ech sinn ebe Perfektionist.
- Kanns du déi Mauer do net rout oder orange usträichen?
- Nee, mir däfen keng grouss Verännerungen am Zëmmer maachen.
- Mir egal, mir gefält et esou.
- Hm, du muss enker jo bei mech kommen.
- Ech hunn eng rout Mauer an der Stuff an eng mof Mauer am Schlofzëmmer.
- E bësse Faarf muss sinn.
- Hey Philippe, great party!
- Thanks, Dani. Nice to see you again.
- Yes, exactly, we’re playing it cool, you’ll fit right in.
- So?
- Yes, I didn’t pay attention, and I’m in the sand-hants for you.
- Actually no one saw me.
- Oh, well, but you’re praised here.
- Yes, and I must say, you really have a nice apartment.
- Thanks, I’m very happy here.
- Have you already introduced your two roommates?
- Yes, two very sympathetic guys.
- Are your neighbors also so nice?
- Yes, so far I haven’t had any problem.
- Then you’re lucky.
- My neighbor makes me laugh.
- He only counts, and then he makes a lot of chaos in the hall.
- Do you then not gossip?
- Well, it’s only the shouts, and the guy still doesn’t fit in.
- But a small mound moves him.
- Then I finally have my peace.
- How much, maybe the next neighbor is even worse.
- Thanks a lot for your optimism.
- Hello you two!
- Hey Jennifer, and?
- Have you seen Philippe’s room yet?
- No, not yet.
- Can I take a look myself?
- Yes, certainly. Come along.
- Now you forgot the game.
- Here is your gift.
- Something hot, hot, subtle.
- Great, thanks.
- I’ll place them best here on the table.
- No, place them on the shelf. There you can see them better.
- And move the table a bit to the left. Then you’ll have more space.
- Okay, boss.
- What can I say? I am a perfectionist.
- Can you not paint that wall red or orange?
- No, we may not make big changes in the room.
- Doesn’t matter, we like it this way.
- Hm, you must come see me.
- I have a red wall in the living room and a blue wall in the bedroom.
- A bit of color must be there.
